<!-- docgen:version=0.2.0 reason: added install-launchd.sh and install-systemd.sh schedulers + scrub.py grew --full / incremental session-scan state; new responsibilities beyond the original pre-commit-only scope -->

## Purpose
The secret-scrub subsystem: a Python scanner (`detect.py` + `scrub.py`) plus three opt-in installers that wire it into a repo as a pre-commit hook and onto the host as a macOS launchd or Linux systemd timer. Catches Solana keypairs, BIP39 mnemonics, base58 keys, PEM private keys, GitHub/OpenAI-style tokens, and `*_SECRET=`-style env assignments before they get committed — and incrementally redacts them out of `~/.claude/projects/*.jsonl` transcripts on a schedule.

## Files
- `detect.py` — pure detection. Exports `find_secrets(text, filename='') -> list[Finding]` and `is_whole_file_secret(text, filename) -> bool`. Owns the regex/heuristic spans for `solana_keypair`, `mnemonic` (12/24 lowercase 3–8-letter words, not BIP39-validated), `base58_key` (87–88 char), `github_token`, `api_key`, `pem_private_key`, `env_secret`. Honours an inline `# secret-scrub: allow` escape hatch per-line. De-overlaps findings, preferring specific kinds over the generic `env_secret`.
- `scrub.py` — CLI. `--staged` scrubs git-staged files (whole-file secrets → `git rm --cached` + append to `.gitignore`; inline → overwrite with `[REDACTED]` and re-`git add`; re-scans after rewrite to detect residual secrets and returns rc=1 if any remain). `--sessions [--full]` walks `~/.claude/projects/` (override via `SECRET_SCRUB_SESSIONS_DIR`), incremental by default via `.secret-scrub-state.json` keyed on `last_run_unix`. Emits a "PRIVATE KEY was exposed and is now COMPROMISED — rotate it" warning when a private-key kind is hit.
- `install.sh` — per-repo pre-commit installer. Case A (an existing global `core.hooksPath/pre-commit` already delegates to `pre-commit-local`): drops a `pre-commit-local` into `.git/hooks/`. Case B: sets this repo's `core.hooksPath` to `tools/secret-scrub/githooks`. Never touches global config. Supports `uninstall`.
- `install-launchd.sh` — macOS launchd user-agent installer. Writes `~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.secret-scrub.plist` running `python3 scrub.py --sessions` every `SECRET_SCRUB_INTERVAL` seconds (default 1800). Idempotent (cmp before write), supports `install|uninstall|status`, honours `DRY_RUN=1`. Logs to `/tmp/secret-scrub.{log,err}`.
- `install-systemd.sh` — Linux systemd-user equivalent. Writes `~/.config/systemd/user/secret-scrub.{service,timer}`. Default interval `30min` (override `SECRET_SCRUB_INTERVAL`), `Persistent=true` so missed runs catch up on wake. Logs to journald (`journalctl --user -u secret-scrub`).
- `test_detect.py` — pytest covering every detector kind, the allow-comment escape, the non-byte-range rejection of fake keypairs, and `is_whole_file_secret` by filename + by content.
- `test_install.py` — hermetic-tempdir tests of `install.sh` Cases A + B, uninstall, and idempotency. Uses `GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL` env override to fake a global hooks config.
- `test_scrub.py` — end-to-end tests in real temp git repos: whole-file unstage+gitignore, inline redaction, clean-pass untouched, private-key rotate warning, and `--sessions` redacts JSONL transcripts (via `SECRET_SCRUB_SESSIONS_DIR`).

## Subdirectories
- `githooks/` — holds the `pre-commit` launcher script that `install.sh` wires in. Fails OPEN on any tool error; only rc=1 (residual secret) blocks the commit. See `githooks/README.md`.

## Dependencies / collaborators
- `git` CLI — `scrub.py` shells out to `git diff --cached`, `git show :<rel>`, `git rm --cached`, `git add`; install scripts use `git config core.hooksPath` and `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`.
- `~/.claude/projects/` — `scrub.py --sessions` reads + rewrites JSONL transcripts in place; state file `.secret-scrub-state.json` sits inside that root.
- `~/.gitignore` (target repo) — `scrub_staged()` appends whole-file-secret paths to it and re-stages.
- `tools/` parent — `CLAUDE.md` codifies the "offer, don't auto-install" contract; preserve that posture when adding scheduler installers.

## Gotchas
- `--sessions` is incremental on file `mtime`: a file written by another process *during* the scan window may slip until the run after next. Cadence is 30 min, so worst-case detection latency is ~1 cycle. Call with `--full` after teaching `detect.py` new patterns, otherwise older files won't be re-scanned against the new rules.
- Mnemonic detection is heuristic (12/24 lowercase 3–8-letter words, NOT BIP39-validated) and will false-positive on ordinary short-word prose. `# secret-scrub: allow` on the line is the escape hatch; don't tighten the regex without a corpus test.
- The pre-commit hook fails OPEN by design (missing `python3`, missing script → exit 0). Only an unscrubbable residual secret returns rc=1. Don't "fix" this — the contract is "never brick commits over a tool bug."

## Working-tree scanner (third leak pathway)

The pre-commit hook only fires on `git commit`. Secrets sitting in
uncommitted or untracked files inside your repos are invisible to it
and to the sessions cron. The third mode closes that gap:

```sh
# Scan one or more repos directly
python3 scrub.py --working-trees ~/code/my-project ~/code/another

# Install scheduled scan (macOS, every 30 min):
./install-launchd-trees.sh             # install + load (creates empty config)
./install-launchd-trees.sh status      # show timer + active repos
./install-launchd-trees.sh uninstall   # remove

# Edit the config to tell it which repos to scan:
echo "$HOME/code/my-project" >> ~/.config/secret-scrub/working-trees.txt
```

Uses `git status --porcelain=v1 -z --untracked-files=all` so the scan
respects `.gitignore` and never touches submodule internals or
deleted files. Skips binary / non-text files automatically.

Config file format: one path per line, `#` for comments, `~` expands
to `$HOME`. Empty config = nothing to scan (safe default).

(Docgen run after this lands will refresh into a clean version that
integrates the new mode into the Files/Gotchas sections properly.)

## .git/config credential scanner (fourth leak pathway)

`.git/config` lives inside `.git/`, which `git status` doesn't track —
so the sessions/staged/working-trees scanners all skip it. A tokenized
remote URL (`https://user:TOKEN@host/...`) parks a live credential in
plaintext there, where `git remote -v` echoes it into any log. This is
the pathway behind a real incident we cleaned up.

```sh
# Audit one or more repos (detect-only; exits 1 if a credential found)
python3 scrub.py --git-configs ~/code/repo-a ~/code/repo-b

# Strip embedded credentials → tokenless URLs (git falls back to its
# credential helper, which is the correct setup)
python3 scrub.py --git-configs ~/code/repo-a --fix
```

Detect-only by default — redacting a token *in* a URL would break auth
(`https://user:[REDACTED]@host` won't authenticate), so the safe move
is to report and exit non-zero (alertable from cron/CI). `--fix`
removes the `user:token@` segment, then the right follow-up is:

```sh
gh auth setup-git   # store creds in the keychain, never in .git/config
```

Also scans `~/.gitconfig` for embedded credentials.
